compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
The completion-verifier agent: adapt the DSO `completion-verifier` to rebar (epic/story/task/bug; bug ⇒ resolved), and the `verify_completion` library operation. Read-only fs + read-only `show_ticket`; PASS/FAIL verdict; FAIL ⇒ findings (criterion + explanation + citation). Prompt-injection hardened (PA1).

## Scope (files)
- `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md` (NEW) + `catalog.json` entry (`completion-verifier`, dimension `completion`). Front-matter vars: ticket_id, ticket_context, repo_path. Findings = failures only; no-criteria ⇒ PASS-with-note in summary; untrusted-input instruction-hierarchy clause.
- `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (NEW): `verify_completion(ticket_id, *, graph=None, …)`; module `__all__`; assemble via `operations._assemble_context`; fenced `<untrusted_ticket_context>`; `mode="structured"`, `output_schema="completion_verdict"`, `extra_tools=_scoped_ticket_tools(allow_comment=False)`; normalize→resolve_citations→`_reconcile` (normalize verdict; FAIL⇔findings)→`validate_structured`.
- `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py`: export `verify_completion`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] verify_completion returns a validated `completion_verdict` for epic/story/task/bug (offline FakeRunner)
- [ ] reconcile: PASS+[]→PASS; FAIL+[]→synthesized finding; PASS+[failing]→FAIL; verdict casing normalized
- [ ] bogus file citation downgraded to source; `criterion` preserved through normalize
- [ ] prompt carries the untrusted-data instruction-hierarchy clause; context is delimited
compiled_state.title: WS-2: completion-verifier prompt + verify_completion operation
